intro: Codaria interrupta, also known as African Plum or Bush Peach, is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Codaria interrupta include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
